Commitments The "What" behind the desir:
Status Tracking for Tables 
Implement a comprehensive status tracking system across core tables within Method. This initiative aims to standardize how statuses are recorded and displayed.
Project (Main Objective): This initiative aims to improve visibility for all users, ensuring that historical status transitions are immutable, and unlocks future product enhancements such as EDA and Swim Lane components.
Success Metrics The expected impact/metr:
- Increased Status Visibility: Users can easily determine the status of records without additional calculations.
- Improved Data Integrity: All status transitions are logged and immutable.
- Enhanced Usability: Users can filter by predefined status values and view historical changes.
- Future Readiness: The system is better structured for EDA, Swimlanes, and app customization.

Narrative The "Why", behind doing this w: Big Bet #5 Ship, Inventory Management Minimum Lovable Product.

1. Ability to set minimum reorder points per item
2. Ability to set minimum reorder quantity per item
3. Displays low-stock items in the Inventory Center with an option to create purchase orders

Narrative The "Why", behind doing this w: Big Bet #5 Ship, Inventory Management Minimum Lovable Product.
Commitments The "What" behind the desir: Backorder Awareness - Customers maintain sales momentum and customer satisfaction by supporting controlled backorders with clear visibility into fulfillment readiness.
Initiatives The "How" needed to achieve:
1. Allows users to override insufficient inventory by placing orders on backorder without interruption to the sales process.
2. Provides visual cues when an item is being backordered during transaction entry.
3. Displays backordered items in a dedicated field, enabling users to monitor pending orders and their status.
4. Ability to manually updated sales orders when inventory is replenished to proceed with invoicing and fulfillment.
Project (Main Objective): JTBD: Ensure that when inventory levels are insufficient to immediately fulfill sales orders, users can easily place items on backorder to prevent lost sales and manage pending fulfillment efficiently.
Success Metrics The expected impact/metr:
X% of sales orders with backordered items have those backorders successfully fulfilled (fully or partially) within 30 days of creation

X% of users agree that “the backorder feature helps me manage stock outs effectively and maintain customer satisfaction”

Narrative The "Why", behind doing this w: Big Bet #5 Ship, Inventory Management Minimum Lovable Product.
Commitments The "What" behind the desir: Item Audit Trail - Customers gain confidence, audit readiness, and operational transparency through a detailed history of item-level changes, quantities, locations, and user actions.

1. Captures a comprehensive log of changes made to inventory records specifically history, transactions, and item details, including adjustments to quantities, locations, pricing, and other key attributes at an item level.

Project (Main Objective): JTBD: Provide users with a detailed, accessible log of all modifications made to inventory items to enhance transparency, ensure accountability, and simplify troubleshooting.
Success Metrics The expected impact/metr:
X% of accounts view the item audit trail at least once per week

X% of users agree that “the audit trail helps me understand inventory changes more clearly”

Narrative The "Why", behind doing this w: Big Bet #5 Ship, Inventory Management Minimum Lovable Product.
Initiatives The "How" needed to achieve:
1. Out-of-the-box easy integration with Katana (no customization required)
2. Real-time 1-to-1 sync between Method and Katana 
3. Real-time inventory count sync (Katana > Method)
4. Sales Order Imports (Method > Katana)
5. Manual sync button to trigger syncs
6. Last sync date for inventory quantities in transactions
7. Sync dashboard to present sync health, status, and history (audit trail)
8. Ability to disconnect/remove integrations
Project (Main Objective): JTBD: In need of advanced inventory management workflows or when using existing external inventory management software (e.g., Katana, SOS), users need an integration that works out-of-the-box with their CRM to avoid time-consuming, error-prone manual data entry.
Success Metrics The expected impact/metr:
X% of integrated accounts complete at least one full sales-to-fulfillment sync loop per week
- Sync Loop: SO Created in Method → SO Syncs to Katana → Line-items Committed in Katana (inventory reserved) → SO Status Updated to “Packed” in Katana (fulfillment step) → Available Stock Updated in Katana → Stock-Level Sync to Method (confirm new on-hand)→ SO Status in Method Updated to “Packed” 

X% of integrated accounts sync at least 10 sales orders per week

Achieve X% success rate for real-time sync events between Method and Katana within the first 6 months of launch
